query: I am folding towels by the washer and want to check the shower without walking down a dark hallway, turn on bathroom light 1 9 minutes from now so I can peek into the stall. I am finishing a laundry load here in the utility room and want soft light for folding and brighter light for sorting, power up utility room dimmer light 1 20 minutes from now at level 40 and increase it to level 70 25 minutes after the previous action

steps.thought: The user wants to schedule three actions. First, I need to verify the current time, then discover the relevant devices (bathroom light 1 and utility room dimmer light 1) and their clusters. I'll start by getting the current time.
First, get rooms to confirm utility_room and bathroom exist.
Now I'll get the devices in the bathroom and utility room to find the correct device IDs.

Bathroom has 'bathroom_on_off_light_1' (on_off_light) and 'bathroom_dimmable_light_1' (dimmable_light). User said 'bathroom light 1' - likely 'bathroom_on_off_light_1' for simple on. Now get utility room devices.
Now I'll get the structure for the dimmable light to find the level control cluster.

I need to schedule three actions: turn on bathroom light 1 at 12:46:44, turn on utility dimmer light 1 at level 40 at 12:57:44, and increase to level 70 at 13:22:44. I'll start by scheduling the first workflow for the bathroom light.
Now schedule the utility room dimmable light actions. First, at 12:57:44, turn it on and set level to 40. Second, at 13:22:44, set level to 70. I'll create two separate workflows.

action_input.answer: I've scheduled: 1) bathroom_on_off_light_1 to turn on at 12:46:44 for peeking into the shower, 2) utility_room_dimmable_light_1 to power on and set to level 40 at 12:57:44 for folding, and 3) increase to level 70 at 13:22:44 for sorting.
